sanskrit:
सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत।
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः॥
transliteration:
sattvānurūpā sarvasya śraddhā bhavati bhārata |
śraddhāmayo 'yaṃ puruṣo yo yacchraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ ||
literal_meaning: According to (one’s) sattva, faith arises in everyone, O Bhārata. This person is made of faith; whoever has that faith, he verily is that.
contextual_meaning: Our beliefs are not chosen—they are symptoms of the mind’s current mode (guṇa). Change the mode and the belief system automatically shifts; hence therapeutic work is less about arguing doctrines and more about elevating the quality of mind.
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive insight—realizing that one’s convictions are transient mental artifacts conditioned by subtle emotional habits, not absolute truths.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media and feeling both righteous anger and smug superiority toward ‘those idiots’ who hold the opposite political view—then pausing to notice how the same platform algorithmically feeds the emotional mode that keeps you engaged.
anchor_text: Your beliefs mirror your mind’s mode; uplift the mode and the beliefs follow.
